theorems.name: Doubly-even closure
theorems.statement: All codewords have Hamming weight divisible by 4

theorems.name: Strict radius-one correction
theorems.statement: Every single-bit corruption of a codeword has a unique nearest codeword; distance ≥2 is not silently healed

theorems.name: Branch existence
theorems.statement: Every nonterminal admissible branch has at least one admissible continuation or explicit terminal rule

theorems.name: Branch count law
theorems.statement: For finite branching factor q, depth-n leaf count is qⁿ unless pruning/terminal rules apply

theorems.name: Branch measure normalization
theorems.statement: For each depth n, Σ_{ℓ∈L_n} μ(ℓ)=1

theorems.name: Born-rule or frequency theorem
theorems.statement: Observed branch frequencies follow |ψ|² or a declared replacement law

theorems.name: Observer memory path theorem
theorems.statement: Each committed observer record is embedded in at least one admissible branch

theorems.name: Branch decoherence
theorems.statement: Macroscopically distinct leaves become effectively non-interfering

theorems.name: Branch ensemble bridge
theorems.statement: A fixed map ℬ_ℓ sends branch ensembles to physical coarse variables

theorems.name: Emergent geometry stability
theorems.statement: Coarse-grained ASH branch ensembles yield stable geometry-like variables

theorems.name: Observable computability
theorems.statement: H(z), P(k), C_ℓ or declared alternatives are computable from fixed ASH equations
